title: cieca porophylla
side_effects: Cieca porophylla can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and dizziness due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to stomach ulcers, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause kidney damage and liver failure in severe cases. Additionally, improper use of cieca porophylla can also trigger allergic reactions, such as itching, rashes, and difficulty breathing.
